Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

It looks like conf-sanity test_32a was skipped during testing:

 "SKIP: No applicable tarballs found"
Patch Set 1: Rebased

Patch Set 2:

I rebased this patch, but I suspect it will still fail testing.  Sarah, could you please look into why the ZFS upgrade tests are not actually being run.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/18348/

Patch Set 2: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/18348/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 2:

Toro has started the test process

Patch Set 2: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/9ff0cca2-2505-11e3-839b-52540035b04c).  Ran 16 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2:

Looks like the conf-sanity.sh test_32[ab] were skipped again:

 == conf-sanity test 32a: Upgrade (not live) == 22:33:43 (1380000823)
 find /usr/lib64/lustre/tests -maxdepth 1 -name 'disk*-ldiskfs.tar.bz2'

 SKIP: conf-sanity test_32a No applicable tarballs found

Not sure why it is checking for "ldiskfs", when the test should be running with "zfs" for the filesystem type.  It also is confusing that it wouldn't then find the ldiskfs images?

It probably makes sense to improve the SKIP message to print the $IMGTYPE value so we can see what is going on. It might also make sense to repeat the "find" without the $IMGTYPE part so that it lists all of the images that are available.

Even more strange, it seems that the tests that were run in
https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/9ff0cca2-2505-11e3-839b-52540035b04c were for ldiskfs, but STILL didn't find the test images. Have these tests been broken for ldiskfs for a long time?  I was going to give this -1 because test_32 was not run, but now I think there is a larger problem that needs to be investigated.

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I found in all of the other tests that are being skipped are running ZFS and should be working properly, for example in:
https://maloo.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/6d5aed80-24a2-11e3-86f9-52540035b04c

 find /usr/lib64/lustre/tests -maxdepth 1 -name 'disk*-zfs.tar.bz2'

 SKIP: conf-sanity test_32a No applicable tarballs found

I guess what else is missing in this patch is a Test-Parameters line that requires a ZFS test run so that we can verify the zfs test is working. 

I'm still not sure why the ldiskfs test images were not found once the ZFS image is added? Maybe it has something to do with how the wildcard is generated for the "find" command?  I hope the debugging suggested in the previous comment will help narrow down the problem.

Patch Set 2:

I am looking into this. The skip issue is cased by the mistake in the Makefile.am, but I hit another issue and will fix them all and push a new one.

Patch Set 8: Fails

from the logs:

running as uid/gid/euid/egid 60000/60000/60000/60000, groups:
 [dd] [if=/dev/zero] [of=/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/t32_qf_new] [bs=1M] [count=20] [oflag=sync]
dd: writing `/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/t32_qf_new': No space left on device
8+0 records in
7+0 records out
7340032 bytes (7.3 MB) copied, 33.9893 s, 216 kB/s
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 60000/60000/60000/60000, groups:
 [createmany] [-m] [/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/t32_qf_] [2]
mknod(/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/t32_qf_1) error: Disk quota exceeded
total: 1 creates in 0.00 seconds: 306.94 creates/second
 - unlinked 0 (time 1380983730 ; total 0 ; last 0)
unlink(/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/t32_qf_1) error: No such file or directory
total: 1 unlinks in 0 seconds: inf unlinks/second
dd: writing `/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/tmp_file': No space left on device
1+0 records in
0+0 records out
0 bytes (0 B) copied, 0.742719 s, 0.0 kB/s
 conf-sanity test_32b: @@@@@@ FAIL: dd failed

Patch Set 8:

I think this is a lustre bug and will file a ticket.

Patch Set 8:

With FSTYPE=zfs:

 dd: writing `/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/tmp_file': No space left on device
 1+0 records in
 0+0 records out
 0 bytes (0 B) copied, 0.742719 s, 0.0 kB/s
  conf-sanity test_32b: @@@@@@ FAIL: dd failed

Dmesg on client:

 LustreError: 8944:0:(vvp_io.c:1079:vvp_io_commit_write()) Write page 1830 of inode ffff88006b1a1138 failed -28
 LustreError: 8950:0:(vvp_io.c:1079:vvp_io_commit_write()) Write page 0 of inode ffff88006b1a1638 failed -28

Are the mdt and ost in disk2_4-zfs.tar.bz2 big enough?

Patch Set 8: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Mark it -1 until the test can pass.  This may be failing due to LU-3522 / LU-2049 causing the ZFS OST to run out of grant during the write.  It may require a larger OST in order to be able to complete the "dd" operation.

Yes, I think 2MB+ is not too bad these days so it should be ok to land. I don't think we will be changing the image file very often after the patch lands.

I'm reluctant to land patches for ZFS unless they are known to pass, since we are very close to getting all of the current tests to pass and enforcing review-zfs test results.

Patch Set 13:

So shouldn't this be blocked on LU-4406?  I think pushing a test patch that we know will fail while trying to get review-zfs to be enforcing isn't the right idea.  We could add this and EXCEPT it for ZFS marking it as waiting on LU-4406?

Patch Set 13:

Nathaniel, LU-4406 is hit only with this patch, which is something happened during upgrade. According to the comments there, it seems some regression happened recently.

parents.subject: LU-1778 libcfs: add a service that prints a nidlist

commit.subject: LU-2687 test: add b2_4 zfs image for conf-sanity test_32a
commit.message:
LU-2687 test: add b2_4 zfs image for conf-sanity test_32a

In order to ensure that we do not break ZFS upgrades
in the future, add 2.4.0 zfs filesystem test image for
conf-sanity.sh test_32a.

Test-Parameters: mdtfilesystemtype=zfs \
ostfilesystemtype=zfs mdsfilesystemtype=zfs \
envdefinitions=SLOW=yes testlist=conf-sanity

Change-Id: Iae560e05b428907409dc7069d30b601b52750cca
Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei3.liu@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7193
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
